question: Compare "time windows" across the set: list all explicit day-based timeframes.

runs.answer:
- Sixty (60) days: Builder warrants doors, windows, electric switches, receptacles, fixtures, caulking, plumbing fixtures, and cabinet work will be free of defects for 60 days [1], [2].
- Sixty (60) days: Builder will repair or replace defects at no charge within 60 days (longer if delays) [12], [23].
- Sixty (60) days: Limited Warranty terminates sixty (60) days after commencement for certain items; claims must be made in writing within sixty (60) days as applicable [19].
- Sixty (60) days: Either party may terminate the Property Management Agreement with sixty (60) days written notice [15].
- Ninety (90) days: Contractor warrants labor for 90 days [7], [11].
- One (1) year: Builder warrants floors, ceilings, walls, internal structural components, plumbing, heating, electric wiring, and roof for one year [8], [13].
- One (1) year: Limited Warranty terminates one (1) year after commencement for certain items; claims must be made in writing within one (1) year as applicable [19].
- 180
